title: 6) Treatment duration
text:
The treatment plan should be set beforehand based on age, past history, general health,
current skin condition, current medication, history of other cosmetic interventions, and red
ﬂags. Do not start the treatment and improvise as you go. The patient usually expects instant
changes and does not want a long-term commitment.
At ﬁrst, start with twice a week for 2 weeks if possible, and change to once a week.
Follow up with the patient for at least 3 months. They should be instructed with daily living
modiﬁcations.
A general treatment plan based on age is shown below. This is a just example. It should be
individually planned.
• Under 35: 5-10 sessions
• 35~45: 10-15 sessions
• 45~55: 15-20 sessions
• Over 55: more than 20 sessions
V. Doran. An Introduction to Facial Revitalisation Acupuncture. The European Journal of Oriental Medicine, 2007 Jan;
16
5(5).

48
Facial  Cosmetic Acupuncture
~ !  ~
49
This  is  the  checklist  for
before  starting  a  cosmetic
acupuncture  session.  Organize
the treatment sessions based on
these  criteria:  the  duration  of
needling, quantity of needles to
be used, intensity of treatment,
etc. Also, the practitioner may
even cancel the treatment, if any
signiﬁcant red ﬂags come up.
✔(For  female  patients)  If  the  patient  is  having  her
period, or suffering from premenstrual symptoms:
Certain female patients can be very sensitive and
nervous.
✔Excessive Starvation, dehydration, or fatigue:
Needle fainting is more likely to take place.
✔Any active inﬂammatory region, open wound on the
face, or systemic rashes:
Practitioner can just avoid the region, or cancel
the treatment as needed.
✔Any ongoing symptoms from diseases like ﬂu:
Cancellation  of  treatment  is  recommended.
Sometimes, patients want the treatment despite
their conditions, but you had better not. Bleeding
or bruising can last longer. Remember one of the
principles of cosmetic acupuncture is intentional
injury,  and  this  injury  needs  a  good  general
condition to be healed well.
✔Any other medical intervention should be discussed
with doctor:
Cosmetic acupuncture can interfere with dental
anesthesia, Botox, ﬁller, etc.
✔Too emotional:
Cosmetic acupuncture uses a great quantity of
needles and the face is a very sensitive area of the
body.  Therefore,  the  patient  needs  to  be  fully
relaxed before the session starts.
CHECK POINTS
Facial  Cosmetic Acupuncture
~ !  ~
50
PREPARATION OF THE
TREATMENT ROOM
I am afraid of dental treatment, and one of the reasons is the
scary metal device in front of my eyes. In cosmetic acupuncture,
patients may feel in the same way. Even an ordinary needle can be a
giant scary metal device right when in front of the patient’s eyes. It
could make patients nervous and more sensitive to pain. Therefore,
the practitioner needs to make the treatment environment relaxed
and friendly.
First, the treatment room should be warm. Patients should be
relaxed physically and emotionally in order to not to have tension in
their faces. Ask the patients if the temperature is right for them.
Also, relaxing music or aroma can be helpful.
The patient’s posture should be comfortable and not tense. The
neck position is very important, and sometimes, patients tense their
neck to keep their head from moving. Check the neck and shoulders
to see if they are fully relaxed before starting the treatment.
Facial  Cosmetic Acupuncture
~ !  ~
51
* Choice of needle’s diameter and length is based on the patient’s condition:
- Is the patient used to acupuncture?
- How nervous is the patient?
- Purpose of treatment.
- Location of insertion.
- The practitioner’s skill.
* Other equipment needed:
Disposable mask, surgical gloves, sterile alcohol pad, sterile cotton
ball, magniﬁer light, and so on.
The patient's face and the practitioner's face are very close during the
whole treatment procedure. They can sneeze suddenly, or saliva may exit
mouth  unintentionally.  Therefore,  the  practitioner  needs  to  wear  a
disposable mask during sessions.
PREPARATION OF EQUIPMENT
Facial  Cosmetic Acupuncture
~ !  ~
52
Always,  the  top  priority  of  TCM  treatment  is
changing the patient’s lifestyle to an ideal or better way.
Changing what they eat, how they think, or how they
live is sometimes more important than acupuncture or
herbs.  In  TCM,  we  call  this  Yang-Saeng  養生,  which
means 'nurturing the right way of living’.
In cosmetic acupuncture, diet and mental status are
important  as  well.  In  addition,  regular  exercise  and
practicing  the  right  posture  are  essential  parts  of
keeping an ideal or better state of beauty.  Therefore, the
practitioner  should  be  well  prepared  with  relevant
knowledge, awareness of the patient’s current state, and
the ability to coach patients on an individual basis.
Some patients need to eat less, some should consume
more nutrients, some need to ﬁx their posture, some
need to see a therapist, and some may need to change
their working environments. Through an individuali
